problem.title: Clone Graph
problem.description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph. Return a deep copy (clone) of the graph. Each node in the graph contains a value (int) and a list (List[Node]) of its neighbors.
assumptions: The graph is connected and undirected.
Each node has a unique value 1..n.
Return the clone of the given node.

example.output: Deep copy of the same graph
example.explanation: Node 1 connects to 2 and 4. Clone every node and edge such that no original node is reused.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – DFS with HashMap [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use DFS with a HashMap mapping each original node to its clone to handle cycles and shared references. When revisiting a node already in the map, return its clone directly instead of recursing further, which breaks infinite loops and correctly wires up all neighbor references.

complexity.time: O(V + E) where V is vertices and E is edges

solutions.title: Solution 2 – BFS with HashMap [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a queue for BFS and a HashMap to map original nodes to their clones. Start by cloning the source node and adding it to the queue and map. For each dequeued node, iterate its neighbors: if a neighbor hasn't been cloned yet, create its clone and enqueue it. Add the clone to the neighbor list of the current cloned node. O(V+E) time O(V) space.
